chunk_summary: Adolescents adopting vegetarian diets should be screened for eating disorders, especially if symptoms like weight loss, fatigue, or bradycardia are present. Urgent referral is needed for severe symptoms, with a focus on medical stabilization and supervised dietary plans.
learning_objectives: Identify red flags for eating disorders in adolescents adopting vegetarian diets.
Understand the importance of screening for dietary restrictions and body image concerns.
Learn appropriate referral thresholds and management strategies for suspected eating disorders.
Incorporate neutral dietary counseling to ensure adequate nutrition and energy balance.
key_facts: Vegetarianism in adolescents can mask eating disorders, especially when associated with weight loss, amenorrhea, or fatigue.
Core screening questions include intentional restriction, meal skipping, fear foods, bingeing, and exercise compulsion.
Red flags for urgent referral include rapid weight loss, bradycardia, syncope, severe dietary restriction, and psychosocial impairment.
Medical stabilization and specialist involvement should take precedence over dietary counseling in severe cases.
Supervised dietary plans with family involvement are recommended to avoid calorie counting by the adolescent.
Evidence-based supportive care measures are essential
reference_quote: "Screen when the diet change coincides with weight loss, amenorrhea, fatigue, dizziness, bradycardia, obsessive food rules, or escalating exercise, because diet counseling alone can miss an evolving eating disorder."
